default_annotator.report_text:
This starts as a spear phishing email sent to the initial target FIN7 starts their attack with spear phishing emails to deliver malware. The emails are crafted to provoke a reaction from the target, often using emotional triggers such as

The exchange then leads to an initial document delivery that is usually benign sending attachments as part of a spear phishing campaign. The initial document may appear benign but includes tracking mechanisms.

possesses a tracking pixel that allows the actor to verify whether or not the target is opening attachments, and if so can provide initial visibility into the system of the target the document or tracking mechanism provides information about network connections or system details

The second document the attacker sends is usually the one with the actual payload. This document has either a VBA macro or an embedded OLE which persuades the target to run these in order to drop the first stage loaders Exploiting vulnerabilities in client applications to execute malicious payloads.

An adversary may rely upon a user opening a malicious file in order to gain execution.

Adversaries may abuse Visual Basic (VB) for execution.

Spear phishing a target with a document loaded with a malware payload which as of May 2018 has been the Gri"on payload, or a combination of SQLRat and DNSBot historically. sending phishing emails with malicious attachments to target individuals.

To establish persistence, the group then injects a scheduled task to reestablish the C2 connection to Cobalt Strike which historically is run twice a day Creating scheduled tasks to ensure persistence and reestablish connections is covered under this TTP.

From there the adversary loads process injection tools or memory scrapers (commonly mimikatz) and pivots using tools like powersploit, RDP services, and PowerAdmin Exec. Adversaries may inject code into processes in order to evade process-based defenses as well as possibly elevate privileges.

Adversaries may attempt to access credential material stored in the process memory of LSASS.

Adversaries may use Valid Accounts to log into a computer using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
